text.title: Persona Building Agent Workflow
text.summary: Three-stage Haiku/Opus/Sonnet pipeline with silhouette validation, mandatory human gate between clustering and narrative generation.

rule.statement: Run silhouette scoring or the elbow method before fixing k; never accept an agent's arbitrary cluster count choice.
rule.rationale: Agents given free choice of k will pick the number that makes the narrative cleanest, not the number statistically justified by the data. Silhouette score over 0.5 indicates strong clusters; under 0.25 indicates the feature selection needs revision.

rule.statement: Validate cluster stability by running clustering 3 times with different random seeds; if membership changes significantly, the segments are not robust.
rule.rationale: Unstable clusters produce personas that change with each run. Stability is a necessary condition for using personas to drive decisions.

rule.statement: Human gate between cluster identification (Opus) and narrative generation (Sonnet) is mandatory; the researcher names and validates clusters before Sonnet writes about them.
rule.rationale: A bad cluster produces a confidently-written persona that gets adopted without scrutiny. The human gate catches cluster misinterpretations before they become organizational mythology.

rule.statement: Tag each persona attribute as data-derived or assumed; downstream agents must not treat assumed attributes as facts.
rule.rationale: LLMs produce persona narratives that sound authoritative regardless of data quality. Explicit tagging forces honest calibration and makes personas auditable.

rule.statement: Agents generating JTBD statements from behavioral data without qualitative input will fabricate motivations; require citation of a data point or an explicit "inferred" flag.
rule.rationale: Behavioral data tells what, not why. JTBD statements require motivation — which is absent from analytics exports and must come from interviews.

rule.statement: Present personas in a team workshop before use; the act of naming and discussing clusters surfaces assumptions that the data cannot validate.
rule.rationale: Personas are organizational coordination tools. Team discussion reveals when a cluster label is misleading or when two clusters represent the same user type under different names.

text.reference: K-means clustering pipeline with cluster summary
Silhouette scoring and optimal k recommendation
Prompt for generating structured persona JSON from validated clusters
